heading: What versions of TLS does Atlas support?
body:
Atlas requires TLS (Transport Layer Security) connections for all Atlas clusters.
After July 2020, Atlas enabled Transport Layer Security  (TLS (Transport Layer Security))
protocol version 1.2 by default for all new Atlas  clusters
regardless of the MongoDB version.

MongoDB 7.0 and later disabled support for TLS 1.0 where TLS (Transport Layer Security) 1.1+ is available.
You can manually configure TLS (Transport Layer Security) 1.1 or 1.0 by
[Configure Additional Configuration Options](scale-cluster-more-configuration-options).

> **Important:** Beginning July 31st, 2025, Atlas will no longer support TLS (Transport Layer Security)
> 1.0 or 1.1 under any circumstance. Atlas will upgrade all
> clusters to reject attempts to connect with TLS (Transport Layer Security) 1.0 or 1.1.
>
> Any client connections configured for TLS (Transport Layer Security) 1.0 or 1.1 will undergo a service
> outage during this upgrade. To avoid this, set the minimum TLS (Transport Layer Security)
> version of your clusters to 1.2 at your earliest opportunity.

You can read more about timing and reasons for the change from the
[Payment Card Industry (PCI)](https://blog.pcisecuritystandards.org/migrating-from-ssl-and-early-tls)
as well as the
[National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)](https://www.nist.gov/news-events/news/2014/04/nist-revises-guide-use-transport-layer-security-tls-networks).

If you have questions about TLS (Transport Layer Security) support or cannot update your
applications to support TLS (Transport Layer Security) 1.2, please contact Atlas
MongoDB Support.
